page.content: In a study examining perineal descent, a total of 991 cases were analyzed with respect to various conditions. The number of cases for perineal descent levels -1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, and 3.5 were 5, 7, 227, 257, 308, 76, 82, 15, 11, and 2, respectively. Among 566 cases of urinary incontinence, the rates for perineal descent levels -1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, and 3.5 were 80, 42.85, 51.54, 50.19(NS), 60.06(p<0.05), 60.52(NS), 75.60(p<0.001), 66.66, 81.81, and 50. The incidence of faecal incontinence, based on 41 cases, was reported as 0, 42.85, 1.32, 3.50(NS), 4.54(p<0.05), 3.94(NS), 12.19(p<0.001), 6.66, 9.09, and 0 across the perineal descent levels.
